During my three-week psychiatric nursing rotation in the TERATAI ward at Dr. Sardjito General
Hospital, I experienced a significant shift in my perspective toward individuals with mental health
disorders. Previously, I held negative stereotypes and viewed them as frightening individuals.
However, after interacting directly with them, I realized they were not as intimidating as I had
imagined. This experience opened my eyes to the fact that they are also human beings who require
attention, understanding, and support.
This rotation also played a crucial role in developing my empathy toward patients. I learned to
understand their feelings and needs, and to put myself in their shoes. As a result, I became more
attuned to patients' conditions and able to provide better, more humane nursing care. The empathy
cultivated during this rotation will be an essential asset in my future nursing career.

Terima kasih untuk semua perawat teratai, luvv bgtt🥰🫶🏻✨

Very disappointed. Staffs, both nurse and doctors, are very slow, unprofessional, and disrespectful to the patients. Let me explain why (disclaimer: this might not apply to different polyclinics, but hey, let's not discount my experience).

I went to obgyn clinic to get a USG and treatment for my ovarian cyst. I've registered online 5 days before and had an appointment with a doctor. When I came to re-register myself in the desk, the staff denied my reservation and instead asked me to register the regular one. Okay, I can accept this if he gave me an adequate explanation, which of course, he didn't. Instead, he said that the doctor I had an appointment to "is not working there again". Very ridiculous because I can see the nametag of the doctor in the hallway.

Fast forward, I've registered a regular one and waited for my turn. It took almost 5 hours to get me done there. Here's the recap:
09.30 - 10.00 : waiting for checkup by the nurse
10.00 - 13.00 : after waiting for 3 hours, finally consulted with the doctor
14.00 : USG finally taken

Clearly, the staffs are very disrespectful with the patients' time. At the doctor's room, I was also treated badly. The doctor who assigned to check on me suddenly leave without saying a word neither to me or his colleagues in his room (he could say to them to at least take care of me, i.e.). This didn't happen only to me. Another lady who already lying in the bed also had to wait for long because the doctor who was taking care of her suddenly leave.

These all are very disappointing for a big hospital like Sardjito. Please, educate the doctors better on treating the patients better. I was also saddened by the fact that in that waiting room, there were quite a few old women waiting in wheelchair for their turn FOR HOURS. Please, fix your work better and change/revamp the work distribution to be more effective.

All in all, I'll not go here again. I better go to another hospital who knows to appreciate my time.

TL;DR : don't go here if you are the kind of person who really appreciate your time.

Also, a moral value: if you are already here, starting to be upset with the system, wondering if other people had experienced the same as you do and thus scroling through GMaps review, don't be afraid to question the staff everything you need to know as clear as possible. Surely, it was my mistake too who was easily being tossed around here and there.

I accompanied my friend who got sick and was treated here, and overall the experience was really good. The hospital is clean, comfortable, and well-maintained. The room was clean and had good air conditioning, which made it comfortable to stay in.

The registration and check-up process went smoothly without long waiting times. The staff, from security to nurses and doctors, were friendly and helpful, which made everything feel organized and easy. Parking was easy to find, and there are also food stalls and a minimarket inside the hospital, which is very convenient for visitors or families waiting.

Overall, it’s a pleasant and reliable hospital with good facilities and great service.

Never fun to be so far from home and yet sick like a dog! After 3 days stuck in bed at the hotel a local doc suggested I go to the hospital.

Found this one on some expats websites with relatively good reviews, so I went straight to the ER (old building not the new ones as it’s easy to get lost) and I was not disappointed: friendly staff, good doctor, they spoke English and when they couldn’t we used translator apps.

A couple of hours later, I had an IV and some meds and was sent back to the hotel.

So yeah if you’re unlucky enough to find yourself not feeling well in Yogyakarta this is a good place to go.

My father was hospitalised here. Everyone who work there are professionals. Very good in doing protocol as well, I mean of course, it's a huge hospital haha. I heard that this hospital is also famous for being good. There's some business in the hospital as well, like bakery, food court and else, for those who are accompanying a member of families that being sick, you can buy some food from the business there. One thing that I really liked was  that the place is so clean. You can have a check or test for covid too, it's PCR test but usually for those who will stay there as patient like my father, for heart problem. No positive covid tested patient there, only for the negative tested. If you're local people or Indonesian citizenship, you can use BPJS(it's similar to insurance but only available for people with Indonesian citizenship) and there's a verification needed if you choose BPJS for the services/payment method to avoid fraudulent. The parking is large enough, it's underground. Anyway, it's a good hospital, and I can recommend this for you ONLY IF YOU'RE SICK. You don't want to stay in hospital if you're healthy, do you? 😂

This is the best hospital I have experienced in Indonesia. The service is outstanding, from the doctors and nurses to the support staff. We stayed in the hospital for five days for my son's treatment, and we were genuinely impressed by the facilities. They have several rooms that face a mini zoo, which we found interesting and entertaining for the pediatric patients. Furthermore, all visitors to the hospital can enjoy the mini zoo for free. The animals are well-maintained, and the place is clean!

The food options were excellent, dispelling the common belief that hospital food is bland and unappetizing. The nurses and doctors were very caring, regularly checking on my son's condition. The janitor cleaned our room twice a day. We were even pleasantly surprised when a staff member visited our room to pray for my son's healing. This level of service in a hospital truly exceeded our expectations.

Not recommended. I came in with a high fever and flu for 2 days. At night, I intended to do a blood test, but the doctor refused to run it even though blood had already been taken, saying that tests would only be done if I was admitted. The problem is, I had a flight to Malaysia in 2 days, so admission was not possible. The doctor only gave me an IV of paracetamol and prescribed paracetamol tablets along with flu medicine. After arriving in Malaysia, I did a test and was confirmed positive for Influenza A. If the hospital had known earlier, they could have prescribed antiviral medication to reduce the symptoms. For an international hospital of this standard to not do any diagnostic tests is very disappointing!

A Very Pleasant Experience with Drg. Endo, the Dentist.

We made a last-minute appointment for our child’s teeth scaling, just one day in advance, taking a chance since we had never visited Dr. Endo Rizqon Mannait before. The patient being treated for scaling was a child with special needs, so when we registered at admission, we were already prepared for the possibility of being rejected. Thankfully, that didn’t happen.

When we arrived at the examination room, the dentist showed positive vibes, was very talkative, and managed to gain the child’s trust right away. What started as a simple scaling appointment turned into a consultation about a tooth root that was still left behind. Initially, we thought the dentist would order an X-ray and refer us to an oral surgeon. To our surprise, drg. Endo had already planned to extract the tooth right then and there.

Honestly, as parents, we were nervous, fearing the child might have a tantrum. We were almost certain that we would be referred and the procedure would require a hospital operating room with general anesthesia. But, long story short, after about an hour of careful and patient work, the remaining tooth root was successfully extracted—without an anesthetic injection and without a referral to a surgeon. It was all done that very day.

After the procedure, our child was all smiles and didn’t end up holding a grudge against Mr. Dentist. Thank you, Drg. Endo. Very well done!

We would definitely come back for any dental issues in the future.

This hospital really lives up to their title, "international hospital." So I came to some local hospitals for a check up and they were (well, sadly) closed on weekend. I tried looking on another hospitals and found JIH, and then came there for the first time. All the staffs would greet you with a smile and everyone was really kind and helpful. And it is not that expensive at all if you are wondering. The buildings are superb, the interiors are just like hotels and I didn't smell any strong medicine there, if you fear hospitals and their strong smell of medicine, I guess you wouldn't fear any of that here. It is very clean and comfortable. They also play some cool music too. Really worth the visit!

I didn't have a good experience. We used hospital for my child to be born in here. It tottaly ruined my experience as a new parent. Instead to feel a joy my baby is born and go home doctor said they will keep him for observation. After my wife delivered, doctor decided to keep my baby for 3 days and we couldn't take him home, reason cause he vomited probably from the waters which broke quite early night before. Only mother is allowed to visit baby in the babies room, The explanation about it's condition was that it needed an oxygen because breathing was not smooth, after few hours all was fine and he was breathing well. He didnt have to use it anymore. They said he could have got infection from broken waters so they need to give it some antibiotics. There was no 100% assurance, that there is infection. Just in case as he vomitted twice after being born. We agreed to everything of course as we trusted doctor. It made us very stressed to know, that something can be wrong with the baby. They kept me in anxiety and fear that my child might be unhealthy.  When we had time to see it, we didn't notice anything wrong with it. Even though hospital made tests and they said that everything was fine, we still couldn't take him home. We had to take a hotel next to hospital so we were close. It is an extra expense, and extra logistics. We came back to hospital and everything was fine with the baby. So my question is why do you keep giving antibiotics and keep a needles in a tiny baby,  rather than let parents take care and trust in immune system? If we would observe he is unhealthy we could look for extra help. What I know for sure is that antibiotics can ruin immune system and make baby weaker. Antibiotics might have many side effects which can be negative for health. Mothers milk is natural medicine and keep baby healthy and strong. Also how is it that even though we took a package which should include all treatment in hospital, we still were charged extra for things they didn't ask if we want or not? So hospital decide, they are not confirming with you, and ask if you want to pay for extra treatment. They just give you the bill and you have no choice but to pay, and it is not cheap. How convinient for you. Price for delivering baby is like few months of average salary in Indonesia + extras on side. They just rip you off on every corner.  We just kept waiting for some news for hours and kept stressed and prayed a lot. They told us that baby is fine but keeping it for observation. They must give antibiotics no matter baby is all right. If everything is fine with baby why do you inject it with antibiotics?. It is a heartless treatment. There are many other things they made wrong but it would be too long to write. I will never let my wife deliver a baby in hospital. Better find better place, more natural were you feel cared for. It is corporation and everything is a business, they will suck out a lot of money from you and of course bpjs is not working in this hospital. We chose it as it is close to home. They make package but still charge extra... Very convinient for hospital, shame on you RS JIH

WORST service so far for if you're going to get a premium covid test, so today on 18th July 2021, I bought the PCR express package that promised to have result in 3 hours for 2.000.000 rupiah. "a pretty hefty price but its worth it" I thought to myself before getting tested. They started the test pretty late, they told the patients that the test will be starting at 10 a.m but the doctors gave some sort of a consultation to another people for well over 1 hour so it's pretty annoying at this point. I tested at around 11:30 a.m and went back home.
As of the time of this review, I've waited for 7 hours AND IT'S STILL NOT HERE, not even a single message of them informing me that the result will be late. But no, thats not even the worst part of this. During the span of those 5 hours I've actually contacted 2 whatsapp contacts, 2 different call extensions, and 7 times to the call center and ONLY 1 OF THEM GOT THROUGH, but even worse, they left me on read on multiple occasions  and if I don't contact them further they won't even reply.
They were kind enough to offer me a refund so that I get the difference between the express package and the same day PCR package back, but at this point its a little too late,  I didn't sign up for that package, I needed the result to be fast, they promised, they failed on that very promise, and the communication is really really bad on their part. And I still havent even gotten my result yet.

What a poor experience at that hospital. I had a confirmed appointment with a certain Dr via their app supposedly at 14.48 and was asked to be present 15’ prior to that time. I arrived at around 14.30.
After some checking by the confused staff, I was told that my appointment had been rescheduled since the Dr had not arrived and that the consultation would now take place at 1600 and that I should wait. Apparently, the Dr did not bother to inform staff  ahead of time about his delayed arrival. Staff also tried to call and WA the Dr but he did not bother to answer
(I was never notified of the rescheduling on my app).
At 1600, I asked the staff about my appointment only to be told that the Dr is still not present and that there is a further delay by about 20’.
At around 1630, still no Dr present.
At 1645, I decided to leave the premises.
My conclusions: 1. It is highly unprofessional to have patients wait for unreasonable time ; 2. The Dr not seems to care that there are patients waiting for him ; 3. The Dr not answering staff calls ; 4.  Some of the staff is confused, not very helpful and some of them quite unfriendly  giving the impression that they don’t care about patient’s complaints ; 5. Staff barely speaks and understands English. So, unless you can speak some Bahasa Indonesia or are being accompanied by someone who does, you are doomed.
And as another reader commented, a negative review here just gets an automated response asking you to contact them to discuss the matter, without addressing the issues in question.
I do not recommend visiting that hospital.

I was having a terrible cough and headache during my short visit to Yogyakarta yesterday. When I was about to dial Siloam Hospitals Yogyakarta contact number I found on the internet, I didn't expect that I would have such swift response with clear information conveyed in a very friendly way. Great first impression!
I immediately went to its IGD unit when I met three different yet excellent services. First was the lady in the administrative section who kindly explained to me the procedures before I met the doctor.
Second, the doctor himself, who patiently and carefully examined the symptoms and made sure that I have told him all the symptoms I got, and wrote me a prescription right away.
And the third, the staff that brought me all the medications and patiently explain each medicine and its function and dosage.
Thank you for such excellent service you served, Siloam Hospital Yogyakarta.

Visited on February 21, 2024. At the time of my visit, I had been feeling unwell for a week. Dr. Joyo Santoso ordered lab work for me. Majority of my results were fine. Dr. Santoso’s ultimate diagnosis for me was anxiety. I have never had severe anxiety issues in my life and I was persistent on that with Dr. Santoso. I continued to feel unwell and visited Siloam in Labuan Bajo. The doctor there ordered lab work for me again, but this time she tested me for typhoid. It came back positive. Finally, I received a diagnosis 9 days after going to Siloam in Yogyakarta.

The hospital itself was nice and the staff were friendly, but I’m very disappointed I was misdiagnosed.

The security and staff looked puzzled when I took my uncle to the IGD for breathing difficulty.

If I may compars those two hospitals, the service in  Hospital Pa*******h and Siloam is like heaven and the deepest crust of the earth.

I am disappointed, won't recommend it to anyone.

One of the best hospital in Yogyakarta, now Siloam Hospital Yogyakarta has been me and my family go-to hospital.

The hospital is squeaky clean without having that distinctive hospital smell, or disinfectant smell. So, it’s very comfortable for us as visitors or patients to be around in the hospital.

The service is excellent, they don’t differentiate their service whether you’re a general patients, an insurance patients, or even BPJS/KIS patients. They treat all visitors or patients in the same helpful and friendly manner. They also provide free hot tea and cups of water for the visitors.

By their entrance door, they also provide free face masks for those of you who forget to wear mask in urgent situation or those who wear non medical mask.

Their emergency room and staff is also incredibly and very affordable. So if you’re in urgent situation, I highly recommend choosing Siloam Hospital.

Their plus point is they located in the city center for easy access. They’re also located closely to 3 hotels, Grand Mercure, Ibis Adisucipto and New Sahid. It’s perfect for the patients family who doesn’t come from Yogyakarta, yet need to stay close to patient without disturbing the patient itself.

My suggestion is they add more options in food for visitors, other than the bakery in the 1st floor. Because sometime there’s a condition the bakery is empty or their breads are sold out or when the mall hasn’t opened yet, the visitors/patients has no option for food. I also highly suggest that bakery to have non-cash payment method.

I had to wait with a really bad experience given by the security and staff.
The security guard keeps on eyeing me and told me to walk all over to Lippo Plaza because I brought my helmet in. My boyfriend dropped me in since I was need a medical treatment.

I'm not sure what's on their mind, but how was I supposed to know that I should not bring my helmet in and how could the security guard expect me to walk that far just to drop in my helmet. The security guard can't even give me a valid reason on why shouldn't I bring my helmet in.

The second would be the staff. They were really not friendly when I came there in my home outfit, shivering and in a huge need for medical treatment. Not even saying sorry nor even thank you for me. This treatment is definitely different when I came here the other day looking rather well-dressed for a flu shot, this is an utter disappointment since I have always relied on to Siloam Hospitals when I'm in another city. I would definitely not gonna go to any Siloam hospitals ever again.

TL;DR : If you look broke, don't even think of going in this hospital, they'll treat you like you're nothing.

I went to this hospital for taking COVID-19 swab /PCR test. I have registered via their website and paid with CC (It was clearly written in their website about the price and payment methods). I got the notification about the order number and payment status.
When I tried to call them for test preparation, they was confuse about my registration and asked me to send the proof of payment through their WhatsApp. I told them several times that I paid the test with credit card (not via bank transfer) and sent both my payment status and order number. Then they asked me to bring those proofs and showed to the staff before taking the test.
On the day that test was taken, I brought all proofs that they asked for, but again they still asked me to show the bank transfer receipt ( I explained everything to the staff and showed the proofs).  Well, my point here is why don't they check their system and put my order number? Are the staff familiar with the system? To be honest, it was uncomfortable experience for me with this hospital even they are big chain hospital in Indonesia.

Bethesda Hospital is one of the oldest hospitals in Yogyakarta. Although the building is quite old, it is very well-maintained, especially in terms of cleanliness. All the staff are very friendly and have a good service mind. They provide heartfelt service. The visiting hours at this hospital are quite strict. Visitors can only visit patients at specified times. When visiting hours are over, security guards will remind visitors to leave the room so that patients can rest comfortably. Inside the inpatient area, there is a minimarket that seems to be open 24 hours a day because when I passed by it in the early morning, they were still operating. This is great to support the comfort of patients and their families. There are 2 parking areas, the first one is outdoors and the second one is in the basement with one exit. Then there is a canteen with quite complete food stalls. Overall, this hospital is quite comfortable for patients or visitors.

RUANG B: Outstanding Chemotherapy Care Experience

I recently underwent chemotherapy treatment at Ruang B and I'm truly grateful for the exceptional service provided. From the moment I arrived, I was met with genuine hospitality and friendly, compassionate staff who made a difficult process feel much more manageable.

The nursing team was attentive and professional, always ready to assist with care and comfort. The room was clean, peaceful, and very comfortable, making it easier to rest and recover. I especially appreciated the thoughtful layout and cozy atmosphere — it truly felt like a healing space.

The food service was excellent, with meals that were not only nutritious but also tasty and well-prepared. Every detail, from daily routines to medical handling, was done with great care and efficiency.

I sincerely thank the entire team — doctors, nurses, and support staff — for their outstanding dedication. Your warmth and professionalism made all the difference.

Highly recommended for anyone seeking quality cancer care in a supportive and caring environment.

Got cancer? Choose Ruang B Bethesda Hospital in Yogyakarta for your chemotherapy.

I had my gallbladder removal surgery at Bethesda Hospital Yogyakarta and stayed for 5 days at the Hibiscus Pavilion. Honestly, I’m very grateful for the whole experience.

The BPJS system here was processed smoothly and without hassle. The staff guided me clearly through every step, from registration to discharge, which really helped take a load off my mind during the process.

The doctors and nurses were professional, kind, and very attentive. Especially the nursing team at the Hibiscus Pavilion — they looked after me 24/7 after the surgery, always checking in, making sure I was comfortable, and quickly responding whenever I needed anything.

Everything, from administrative matters to daily care, was handled well and with care. I truly appreciate the service and would gladly recommend Bethesda Hospital to anyone looking for good medical care with a personal touch.

I was a patient here from Aug 31 - Sep 17, 2022 due to a very bad/severe fracture in my right knee while on vacation. I was extremely satisfied with the medical facilities - all the way from the time I was brought into ER - to my surgery and post-op rehabilitation till I was declared fit-to-fly back to India. I can only say great things about the overall experience, and especially about the staff - whether it was my surgeon/doctor (Dr.Yudha), the nursing staff in Dahlia/Hibiscus wards, Mr.Nova from admin or even the housekeeping/catering staff. Thanks to the prompt medical care and attention by the team, I am well on my way to recovery!

I must admit that language was sometimes an issue but Google Translate worked fairly well and I truly appreciate the good folks at Bethesda to keep us comfortable, smiling and positive in what was otherwise an extremely stressful situation for my wife and I.

Terima Kasih! 🙏🙏🙏

Best medical facility in Jogja. There are three sections all of which you will visit. On arrival register at one of the desks in the entrance hall or walk to your right and register in the casualty ward it's quicker. Also nothing is in English although they are really friendly and willing to help. I suggest taking a local with you to cut through a frustrating registration. Go then to your designated waiting area and be prepared to wait ..... And wait!  Once your physician has seen you the inevitable multi bill is given for consultation and medication for which you go to another waiting  area to be called out after paying at the booth first. Put aside most of the day and hope you don't have a true emergency. The quality of physician and facilities are good generally although my ear condition was not resolved until I went to Singapore. Best o luck!

destination: Rumah Sakit Umum Daerah (RSUD) Kota Yogyakarta
text:
Questionable work ethics, making patients wait for around an hour in average. If you're unlucky and registered for a particularly slapdash, lazy doctor....expect dodgy diagnosis, or waiting for two hours until the doctor in question appear. My personal record was 3 hours waiting for the doctor to come, despite the official hours of 9 to 13 the doc had finally show his face around 12.15.

Also, doctors tended to hit or miss. Some are the best around here, other makes costly mistakes that are not suppose to be made in this field.

On the plus side, it is cheap as hell. And the non-medical staff, especially guys and girls in PR, are very good.

Visiting Piyungan, I suddenly felt a heavy feeling in my chest and dizzy. My hands and feet felt numb. I remembered I had the number of Doctor Fatma who had helped my husband before. Because he had already gone home, I was called by Doctor Kurnia who was on duty at the time. At 2:02 PM, I arrived at the emergency room and was greeted by Dr. Kurnia and the nurses. I was treated very well. They weighed me, took my blood pressure, recorded my heart, and gave me medication and vitamins under the BPJS program. Thank you, Doctor and the medical staff at the Piyungan Community Health Center who treated me with sincerity, not just expertise. In your alert hands and empathetic hearts, anxiety turned into hope, and fatigue felt lighter. Your concern is the most soothing medicine. Best wishes.

Piyungan Bantul Community Health Center is very close to Jalan Jogja Wonosari. Check the hours of service for the clinic or check-in process in my photo.
Health protocols at this community health center are enforced: you must wash your hands when entering, and you must also wash your hands when leaving. There are plenty of handwashing stations inside. Temperature checks are done at the front desk, and there's also a staff member at the front desk.

I've been here several times, and the service is quite good, especially during off-peak hours. If you need a check-up, there's usually a long line, so be patient. The queue for medication can sometimes be long, as there's a queue.
There's a TT service here to complete your pre-wedding application requirements. The community health center sometimes holds events for pregnant women or mothers of toddlers.
Mandatory immunizations for toddlers are provided monthly. When I was immunizing my baby at the community health center, it seemed the nurse intern was the one administering the injection. The nurse was still young, so my child had a scar after the injection. If the person giving the injection is a woman, the injection site is safe.
Rapid tests are free for pregnant women in Piyungan. There's a minimum gestational age, which will be announced via the pregnant women's WhatsApp group. ANC services are also available at the community health center.
Before the coronavirus, I visited relatives who were hospitalized. The place was quite spacious, but the facilities in the rooms were basic, though I don't know about them now.

In front of the community health center, there's adequate parking for motorbikes. There's a parking attendant who charges 2,000 Rupiah. But for car users, you'll have to find a spot on the main road. Thank you.

Tugu Yogyakarta Station offers a comfortable, strategic, and culturally rich travel experience. Overall it delivers excellent facilities, well integrated transportation access, and a charming atmosphere for heritage lovers. A must visit gateway when arriving in Yogyakarta.

Tugu Yogyakarta Station offers a comfortable, strategic, and culturally rich travel experience. Overall it delivers excellent facilities, well integrated transportation access, and a charming atmosphere for heritage lovers. A must visit gateway when arriving in Yogyakarta.

Busy, central station with good connections

Stasiun Tugu is one of the main train stations located right in the heart of Yogyakarta. Most intercity trains depart from or arrive here, including the train to the airport, so it's a really important hub for travelers.

It’s almost always busy, especially on weekends or during the holiday season, so be prepared for the crowd. One thing I find a bit confusing is figuring out which platform to go to when entering from the south gate, it's not always very clear (haha), so it might take a few minutes to get your bearings.

Overall, it's a convenient and well-connected station, especially if you're exploring Java by train!

I'd love to explore the entire Indonesian rail system, but I'm thinking I'd need to chill a good bit to make that work. Always good to know where you are and where you're going. :)

Lempuyangan Station is one of the main train stations in Yogyakarta, Indonesia, and is located in Bausasran, Danurejan.
Facilities include a large, Class B station with 11 train tracks. It serves mixed-class and economy intercity trains, agglomeration trains, and the Yogyakarta Commuter Line.
Self-service check-in for boarding passes is also available, along with 24-hour service, restrooms, a prayer room, a children's play area, food stalls, and more.

Several trains stop at the station, including the economy intercity trains: Bogowonto, Gajahwong, Sri Tanjung, Pasundan, Kahuripan, Bengawan, Jaka Tingkir, and Logawa; the Yogyakarta Commuter Line (Yogyakarta-Palur); and the Joglosemarkerto agglomeration train (Solo Balapan-Semarang Tawang).

destination: Office of the Mayor of Yogyakarta
text:
This morning, I went to the City Hall, intending to take an ID card that had been corrected, but it turned out,  it wasn't done.  It seems that there is indeed an effort towards fast, precise service but it is not yet supported by adequate resources.
The Population and Civil Registry Service @Dinas DukCaPil servers often hang, take long to load and are unresponsive.
Hopefully there will be improvements soon so that the goal of the Yogyakarta City Government towards qualified services ❤  JSS Jogjakarta Smart Services will be realized soon 🍻 CHEERS 👏
Regarding the situation, the current situation at City Hall compared to when I was active 9 years ago, is very... very different.
The building is physically well maintained but also seems to be ^getting denser.^
Hopefully this density is due  ❤🌹  to the work of serving the community which is increasingly diverse in type and evidence that community members really take advantage of public service facilities at City Hall. 🙏🏻 🌈 🔝
Bravo to our Jogjakarta City Government Staff and keep on serving the spirit. 👏🙏🏻🌷

text:
Although I've been to City Hall several times to apply for my ID card (I'm a newcomer who, thank God, has been a city resident since 2018), to apply for TPA assistance, and to participate in a cycling event, this was my first time at the Mayor's office.

The health protocols are quite strict. In addition to temperature checks and mandatory masks, visitors must be vaccinated. Those who haven't been vaccinated will be vaccinated on the spot. The city government will also assist Islamic schools (Madrasah Diniyah), Islamic boarding schools (Ponpes), and TPA/TPQ (Islamic boarding schools) that wish to provide free vaccinations.

Thank God, I've never had any issues with population administration. BPJS is also available here. In fact, I'm very pleased with the service; it's hassle-free, and the card will be delivered to my home. Thank you, Mr. Mayor and Deputy Mayor!! 🤩🤩🤩🤩🤩🤩🤩

text: The Yogyakarta City Government Center complex. Numerous government buildings are located within the complex. Residents simply need to find the building that best suits their needs. There's a large grassy field and a large mosque near the entrance. There's also a free children's playground, but unfortunately, it's poorly maintained. Parking for motorcycles and cars is free, but it's often full due to the large influx of residents and employees. You have to be smart about finding a free parking spot or arriving early. Occasionally, there are major celebrations or national holidays that can delay service, but it's not a problem. Service to the public is truly fast and clear.

Supporting facilities are quite complete, including a mosque, parking lot, canteen, bank, ATM, and more. Processing ID cards, family cards, permits, and paying taxes is all available.

destination: Office of the Mayor of Yogyakarta
text:
Came here just to print my new ID but ended up with nothing just because JSS Apps waited for 3 hours. Asking 3 different workers here, all of them didn't come up with solution, everything should be done within JSS Apps.

I shared JSS menu here so anybody can also know how to do it.

Go to "Applying for KTP" menu, then you run into Dukcapil website Link, then they ask your registration number (which I don't have since the beginning, because I haven't been told what to do in the first place after all, hahaha) so I was anxious to consult in JSS counter which I had number 19 for queue, guess what, there's no number counter, and some "Ibu-Ibu" just crossed the queue line because they knew the clerk. And so the queue line goes crazy forgetting Health Protocol.

Just so you know, first of all, before printing your ID card, there's a drive-thru system, but some of it had to take place in the "Mall Jasa Pelayanan Publik" (Public Service Mall) where I came all the way here.

Second, my new ID is needed in the Village Ward/Kelurahan to process my newborn birth certificate, which I can't do either.

In my deepest heart, I'd please, you are a civil servant, so please help the people of Jogja with ease.
Administration like this should be straightforward, and the information provided should be consistent.

Every time I meet with an officer, there's always a requirement missing. If I could give it a 0 for further improvements, I would, all for the convenience of all residents. A 1 for the building, interior, comfortable design, numerous gardens, attractive and shady layout, with a score of 8 for all. For poor and complicated service, and an unprepared application, it all makes it meaningless.

Not everyone has ALL DAY to take care of just one thing here. Thank you.

destination: Office of the Mayor of Yogyakarta
text:
The Yogyakarta Mayor's Office is the office complex where the Mayor of Yogyakarta works. In addition to the Mayor's Office, several government offices are located here, comprising 48 regional government agencies (SKPD), including 13 government agencies, 10 Regional Technical Institutions (LTD), one other institution established by law, 9 divisions, and 14 sub-districts. The Manpower Office and the Population and Civil Registration Office are located in this complex. However, I haven't received any updates regarding the relocation of these offices to improve public service efficiency.
As the capital of the Yogyakarta Special Region (DIY) province, Yogyakarta has a population of over 2 million.

Service at this office is quite good, although a trending topic on Twitter was verbal sexual harassment of a guest of the Manpower Office processing an AK1 by a Public Order Agency (Satpol PP) officer, whose office is across from the mayor's office. Hopefully, this kind of incident will not happen again, as the people of Yogyakarta are known for being polite, friendly, and respectful. Perhaps that's also the attraction that makes many local and international tourists linger over their holidays in this city. Welcome to Yogyakarta.

destination: Special Region of Yogyakarta Governor's Office
text:
First of all, thank you to the Kepatihan (Regional Office of the Yogyakarta Regional Secretariat), especially the Yogyakarta Regional Secretariat's legal bureau, for allowing me to work here. Thank God, I was able to work here with your guidance.
This governor's office is one of the offices with historic buildings, especially the Kepatihan ward. This office still preserves this historic building, is well-maintained, and has undergone renovations, so the building still looks great. It was a very exciting experience to work here, and to learn about the work programs at Kepatihan. The place is spacious, the service in all the bureaus is excellent, and the facilities are complete, making us feel comfortable during our visit.

A great public service location 👍
Free and ample parking

The following agencies operate here under the Ministry of Finance:
- Ministry of Finance Representative Secretariat
- KPPN
- KPKNL
- LPSE
- Tax Court

There is a prayer room in the main building. To the west, close to the Secretariat of the Ministry of Finance Representative Office.

The building layout is solid.
There are fire hydrants.

The road is painted to differentiate between motorized vehicles and pedestrians.
However, some pedestrians still don't walk in the designated area, specifically the red area.
Perhaps they're unfamiliar with it or simply don't care.

The location sign is good.
Clearly legible and easy to understand.

There's a park with seating on the west side. The greenery is worthy of emulation by other government agencies.

The entrance is on the north side, and the exit is on the west side.
You can't enter through the front road facing the Heroes' Cemetery.
Perhaps this is to avoid traffic jams, as there's also a TransJogjakarta bus stop in front and the traffic is heavy.
So, TransJogja bus riders have to walk to the back.

A free family educational park on the West side of Gajah Wong River in a middie class residential complex in Pandeyan, Umbulharjo District, Yogyakarta City. Small kids can play at the playgrounds. The best RTPH (Public Green Open Space) I've ever visited in Yogyakarta.

Free Wi-Fi
Free Parking
Free Toilet

Don't worry, locals in front of the park sell snacks and drinks at very affordable prices.

The most spacious area of ​​any RTPH. There's also a play area for people with disabilities and it's very child-friendly. If possible, bring mosquito repellent or telon oil from home before visiting, or wear long-sleeved clothing and long pants.

Some play facilities just need some renovation, like the slides that are starting to have holes and the rusty, faded paint on the metal parts to prevent tetanus and endanger children.

Friday morning I cycled to a park not too far from my house, a park I initially learned about on Instagram. "It turns out there's a nice park in the Gambiran area, Umbulharjo...
🌴🌱 GAJAH WONG EDU PARK
📍 Location for first-timers
If you pass the Gambiran intersection/from Warungboto, continue straight before the PKS office, turn left, go straight, then turn right slightly, then continue straight to the end, then turn right, go straight again slightly, then turn right slightly, and you'll arrive at Gajah Wong EDU PARK.
🏷️ Facilities/What's inside Gajah Wong Edu Park?
- Children's play area (interactive, motor skills)
- Fitness area
- Protected wildlife
- Several rare trees
- Gazebo
- Mosque
- Observation post
- Pool
- Restrooms
- Parking area
- Meeting point
- Municipal Wi-Fi
- Green Village Gambiran
⏲️ Open: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM
🔖 Admission 🆓
🛵🚗 Parking is limited, as the park is located in a residential area. Cars can park on the side of the road or in the parking lot next to the mosque. Motorcycles can park along the park...
ÑB
Don't expect too much when you visit. Some of the children's play equipment is still in good condition, the seating area is still quite nice and well-maintained, the park is always cleaned by the man from the environmental department, etc.
"This is just a park on the outskirts of town, so don't expect too much, but it's still worth a visit. It's a place to play with the kids, a place worth visiting with the family. Hope you like it, guys 😀🙏

Gajah Wong Educational Park is a comfortable green open space in the heart of Yogyakarta. It's peaceful, surrounded by trees, and perfect for relaxing in the afternoon, jogging, or taking the family on a small picnic. Children can play in the playground, while parents can sit in the gazebo or take a leisurely stroll along the designated paths.

In my opinion, this park isn't just a recreational spot, but also a space for community interaction and environmental education. An added value lies in the community atmosphere that frequently holds activities here. Overall, it's well worth a visit if you're looking for a peaceful atmosphere without having to venture far from the city center.

During my time living in Yogyakarta, this has been one of the open spaces in the city I visit most often. Whether you’re looking for a spot to jog, take a walk, have a casual catch-up with friends, or even enjoy a small picnic, Wisdom Park is a great fit for all kinds of visitors. The area is shaded, clean, and generally calm on weekdays. Even on weekends, when more people come by, it still feels pleasant and comfortable. With so many open spaces available in Yogyakarta, this one stands out as a highly recommended alternative, especially if you’re around UGM and UNY area and want something close by.

Wisdom Park UGM is a spot on campus that’s perfect for some outdoor relaxation or exercise. The jogging track around the park is a great place for a morning run or walk, and there are plenty of park benches where you can sit and enjoy the surroundings. The area is quite shaded and green, creating a serene and refreshing atmosphere.

The park also has a lake, which adds to the tranquil atmosphere. However, the last time I visited, the lake was somewhat dirty, which detracted from the overall experience. With a bit more care, this area could become a real highlight of the park.

However, there are some areas that could use a bit more attention. The plants and flowers around the park seem to lack proper maintenance, and with some care, they could make the park even more vibrant. There’s also a lot of space in the park that could be utilized better, perhaps by adding some gym equipment for outdoor workouts.

The facilities, like the toilets, are decent and well-maintained, which is always a plus.

Wisdom Park at Gadjah Mada University (UGM) is a thoughtfully designed open space located within the campus area in Sleman, Yogyakarta. Surrounded by trees and landscaped gardens, the park offers a refreshing contrast to the academic buildings that dominate much of the university grounds. Its layout features wide walking paths, a tranquil lake, benches, and several shaded spots, making it a comfortable place for relaxation, study, or light conversation. Despite being relatively simple in structure, the space is carefully maintained and visually appealing, especially during the early morning or late afternoon when natural lighting enhances its calm atmosphere.

As a student of Universitas Gadjah Mada, Wisdom Park often feels like a quiet retreat from the academic pressures of campus life. Many students come here to read, reflect, or simply rest in between classes. The presence of water and green space helps create a peaceful environment that encourages mindfulness and serenity. While not necessarily a major tourist attraction, it serves its purpose well as a community green space, supporting the university’s educational philosophy of balance between knowledge, nature, and well-being.

However, its popularity among students and visitors can sometimes lead to crowding during weekends or special events, slightly diminishing the quietness it normally offers. Facilities such as restrooms or designated reading corners are somewhat limited, so visitors looking for extended stays may need to plan accordingly. Nonetheless, Wisdom Park remains a pleasant and valuable addition to the UGM campus, fostering a deeper appreciation for nature within an academic setting.
